title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your college has asked you to write a report on the effectiveness of a recent careers fair held on campus. Your report should assess the event's organisation and content, evaluate how useful it was for students seeking employment, and make recommendations for future events.
tasks.modelAnswer:
**Report on the Annual Careers Fair**

**Introduction**
This report aims to assess the organisation and value of the careers fair held on 14th March, attended by approximately 400 students and 35 employers. Data was gathered through an online survey distributed to attendees following the event.

**Organisation and Content**
The fair was generally well-organised, with clear signage and a printed directory of exhibitors. However, several students reported difficulty locating specific companies due to the layout of the main hall. The range of industries represented was broad, covering finance, technology, healthcare and the creative arts, which was widely appreciated.

**Student Experience**
The majority of respondents (72%) found the event useful, particularly valuing the opportunity to speak directly with recruiters. Nevertheless, a significant minority felt that many employers were only interested in final-year students, leaving first and second-year attendees with limited benefit.

**Recommendations**
Based on the findings, the following improvements are proposed for future events. First, a dedicated zone for students at different stages of their studies would ensure all attendees benefit equally. Second, pre-registering students for one-to-one sessions would reduce waiting times and improve efficiency. Finally, incorporating a CV review station staffed by careers advisors would add considerable practical value.

**Conclusion**
Overall, the careers fair achieved its core objectives but could be significantly enhanced through targeted structural improvements. Implementing the above recommendations would maximise engagement and outcomes for all participants.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement for a summer internship programme at a large marketing company. Write an email to the internship coordinator expressing your interest, explaining why you are a suitable candidate, and asking for further details about the application process and working conditions.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Subject: Enquiry Regarding Summer Internship Programme

Dear Ms Harrington,

I am writing to express my interest in the summer internship programme advertised on your company's website. As a second-year Marketing student at Westbrook University, I believe this opportunity would be an invaluable complement to my academic studies.

I am particularly drawn to your company's reputation for innovative campaigns and its collaborative work culture. Over the past two years, I have developed strong skills in market research, data analysis and content creation through both coursework and a part-time role at a local media agency. I am confident that these competencies would allow me to contribute meaningfully to your team from an early stage.

In addition to my practical experience, I have consistently demonstrated the ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines, a skill I understand to be essential in a fast-paced marketing environment. My academic record reflects both my commitment to the field and my capacity for independent analytical thinking.

I would be grateful if you could provide further details regarding the application process, including any required materials beyond the standard CV and cover letter. I would also appreciate information about the expected working hours, whether the internship is remunerated, and the possibility of remote or hybrid working arrangements.

Thank you sincerely for considering my enquiry. I look forward to hearing from you and hope to have the opportunity to discuss my suitability for the programme in more detail.

Yours sincerely,
Alex Morgan

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have recently attended a workshop called 'Launch Your Career: Skills for the Modern Job Market'. Write a review for your college magazine assessing the content and delivery of the workshop, commenting on how useful it was for students, and recommending whether others should attend.
tasks.modelAnswer:
**A Workshop Worth Your Saturday Morning**

Attending a careers workshop on a weekend requires some convincing, but 'Launch Your Career: Skills for the Modern Job Market' more than justified the commitment. Held over four hours at the city conference centre, this event attracted around sixty students from across several local universities.

The session was structured around three core areas: personal branding, interview technique, and navigating online job platforms. Each segment was led by a different industry professional, which brought welcome variety to the delivery. The personal branding module was particularly impressive, challenging participants to craft a compelling professional narrative rather than simply listing qualifications. Practical exercises were woven throughout, keeping energy levels high and ensuring that concepts were immediately applied rather than merely absorbed passively.

The interview simulation was arguably the highlight of the day. Participants were paired with strangers to practise responses to competency-based questions before receiving feedback from a seasoned recruiter. While initially daunting, this exercise proved enormously instructive, exposing common habits — such as vague answers or poor eye contact — that candidates are rarely aware of.

If there is one criticism, it is that the final module on online platforms felt somewhat rushed, with insufficient time dedicated to LinkedIn optimisation strategies that many students specifically wanted to explore.

Overall, this is a workshop I would wholeheartedly recommend to any student approaching graduation. The practical focus, experienced facilitators and interactive format make it considerably more valuable than a standard lecture on employability. Book early — places fill rapidly.
